title: Modi and Macron Jointly Inaugurate Bharat Innovates 2026 in Nice
lede: Prime Minister Narendra Modi and French President Emmanuel Macron jointly inaugurated Bharat Innovates 2026, a three-day deep-tech innovation summit in Nice showcasing 120 Indian start-ups and 20 institutes across 13 technology pillars to 350+ global investors.

sections.context: Bharat Innovates 2026 represents a strategic platform for India to project its deep-technology capabilities to the global investment community. The three-day event at the Palais des Expositions in Nice brings together Indian innovators, researchers, and start-ups with international venture capital and investment funds, positioning India as a serious player in critical technology sectors. The participation of 350 top investors and venture capitalists from across the world underscores the commercial significance of this initiative. Modi's presence alongside Macron signals French endorsement of India's innovation ecosystem and reflects the deepening bilateral commitment to technological cooperation.
sections.threads: Modi emphasized that India and France have been 'steadfast partners working to address global challenges,' situating Bharat Innovates 2026 within a broader strategic framework. The Prime Minister specifically highlighted [[india-fr-modis-paris-gambit-france-becomes-indias-solar-multiplier-in-2018|the International Solar Alliance]], demonstrating continuity in Franco-Indian environmental and clean energy collaboration. He also referenced ongoing [[india-fr-india-france-innovation-roadmap-2030-strategic-partners-2026|Indo-French initiatives in Artificial Intelligence and Indo-Pacific stability and security]], indicating that technology cooperation extends beyond commercial innovation into defense, sustainability, and geopolitical coordination. The event occurs within the context of [[india-fr-modi-and-macron-jointly-launch-bharat-innovates-2026-in-2026|the India-France Year of Innovation]], suggesting this is part of a coordinated multi-year engagement cycle.
sections.signal: The scale of investor participation—350 venture capitalists and investment funds—will determine whether Bharat Innovates 2026 translates into sustained capital flows into Indian deep-tech start-ups. Analysts should monitor whether the 120 participating start-ups secure funding commitments and establish European market entry pathways in the months following the summit. The presentation of 20 'Institutes of Excellence' across 13 technology pillars suggests India is positioning itself across defense-relevant, climate-critical, and frontier technologies; watch for announcements on research collaboration frameworks between Indian and French institutions. The visibility given to artificial intelligence and Indo-Pacific security themes indicates these will remain priority areas in the [[india-fr-india-france-innovation-roadmap-2030-elevating-strategi-2026|India-France Innovation Roadmap 2030]] and should be tracked for concrete partnership agreements.
